channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bill Maher critiques Trump’s handling of Iran conflict while supporting overall strategy
item.description: Comedian Bill Maher commented on the Trump administration’s handling of the ongoing conflict involving Iran during an appearance on the podcast “Hot Mics With Billy Bush.” While Maher stated that he g...

item.title: Vintage Photos Showcase Mid-Century Curtain Design Trends of the 1950s and 1960s
item.description: The article explores the popularity and stylistic importance of curtains during the 1950s and 1960s, a period described as the “Golden Age of Curtains.” According to the feature, window treatments bec...

item.title: Trump sparks controversy after warning Oman over Strait of Hormuz remarks during Cabinet meeting
item.description: During a Cabinet meeting held at the White House, U.S. President Donald Trump made controversial remarks regarding the strategic Strait of Hormuz and the role of regional countries, including Oman. Wh...

item.title: Glenn Kirschner Questions Pam Bondi’s Return to Trump Administration and Links It to Epstein File Controversy
item.description: A new report from Raw Story highlights comments made by former federal prosecutor and 'Justice Matters' podcast host Glenn Kirschner regarding Pam Bondi’s return to President Donald Trump’s administra...

item.title: UEFA Five-Year Coefficient Keeps Manchester City Above Arsenal in European Rankings
item.description: Arsenal may have ended their long wait for a Premier League title, but the club is still ranked below Manchester City and Liverpool in UEFA’s latest European club rankings. The rankings are based on U...

item.title: Trump DOJ Removes Online Records Related to January 6 Convictions and Pardoned Rioter
item.description: CNN anchor Jake Tapper criticized the U.S. Department of Justice after the Trump administration removed online DOJ records connected to individuals convicted for their roles in the January 6 Capitol a...

item.title: Report Claims Trump Did Not Meet Injured Iran War Soldiers During Walter Reed Visit
item.description: A report by CBS investigative journalist Michael Kaplan alleges that President Donald Trump did not meet with 14 U.S. soldiers injured during the conflict in Iran while visiting Walter Reed National M...

item.title: Former Border Patrol commander criticizes proposal to remove CBP from major airports over sanctuary city disputes
item.description: A former Border Patrol commander under the Trump administration, Gregory Bovino, has publicly criticized a reported proposal by Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin that would remove Customs a...

item.title: Expert disputes Marco Rubio’s claim about U.S. deportation agreements with “safe countries”
item.description: During a recent Trump administration cabinet meeting, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio claimed that the United States has secured deportation agreements with approximately 20 countries around the w...

item.title: ATCL to Restart Passenger Flights to Chato Airport From June 2026
item.description: Shirika la ndege la Tanzania, ATCL, linatarajiwa kurejesha safari za abiria kwenda Uwanja wa Ndege wa Chato mkoani Geita kuanzia tarehe 2 Juni 2026. Taarifa hiyo ilitolewa bungeni na Naibu Waziri wa U...

item.title: Hamas confirms death of military commander Mohammed Odeh in Israeli air strike on Gaza City
item.description: Hamas has confirmed that Mohammed Odeh, ambaye alikuwa kamanda mpya wa tawi la kijeshi la kundi hilo, ameuwawa katika shambulio la anga la Israel lililolenga jengo la makazi ndani ya Gaza City. Kwa mu...

item.title: PNP-ACG reports 32 arrests and 276 online libel cases referred for prosecution in early 2026
item.description: The Philippine National Police Anti-Cybercrime Group (PNP-ACG) reported that 32 individuals were arrested for alleged online libel violations between January 1 and May 15, 2026. Aside from the arrests...

item.title: Sokoto Government Plans New Law Against Informants Supporting Banditry
item.description: Governor Ahmed Aliyu of Sokoto State don announce say him administration dey prepare a new bill wey go punish people wey dey secretly assist bandits with information. The governor talk this one during...

item.title: Former Federal Judges Seek to Reopen Trump v. IRS Case to Challenge $1.776 Billion Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: A group of 35 former federal judges has filed a legal motion to reopen the Trump v. IRS lawsuit, aiming to invalidate a settlement agreement that established a $1.776 billion 'Anti-Weaponization Fund....

item.title: Over 700,000 Children Lose SNAP Benefits Following Federal Policy Changes and Funding Reductions
item.description: A recent analysis highlights a significant decline in the number of children receiving food assistance through the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) following the implementation of a ma...

item.title: Rare 1960s Photos Highlight Cilla Black’s Rise as a British Pop and Television Icon
item.description: The article revisits the early career and cultural impact of British singer and television personality Cilla Black through a collection of 35 vintage photographs from the 1960s. Before becoming one of...

item.title: Republican lawmaker dodges questions on Ken Paxton scandals during conservative radio interview
item.description: During a recent appearance on The Charlie Kirk Show, Rep. Brandon Gill (R-TX) was pressed by host Andrew Kolvet about the political and ethical controversies surrounding Texas Attorney General Ken Pax...

item.title: Former Homeland Security official criticizes new federal worker speech restrictions and raises legal concerns
item.description: A former Homeland Security official, Miles Taylor, has criticized a new Trump administration policy that seeks to restrict what federal employees can say about their work, warning that it could repres...

item.title: How Siouxsie Sioux Became a Defining Figure of Goth Music and Fashion in the 1980s
item.description: The article looks back at the influence and evolution of Siouxsie Sioux, lead singer of Siouxsie and the Banshees, during the 1980s. Born Susan Ballion in 1957, Siouxsie first emerged from the British...

item.title: Matatu Workers Union Warns of Nationwide Strike Over Low Wages and Fuel Crisis
item.description: Matatu workers in Nairobi and other urban centres have threatened to stage industrial action over poor pay and the rising cost of fuel. The warning was issued by the Matatu Workers Union (MWU) togethe...
